task_completion.description: Configure Wazuh to monitor the target critical application configuration file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal showing a text editor (like nano or vim) or a command (like sed/echo) adding the path '/var/ossec/etc/critical_app.conf' to the <syscheck> section of the Wazuh configuration file (ossec.conf).

task_completion.description: Enable real-time monitoring and content diffing for the target file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the attributes 'report_changes="yes"' and 'realtime="yes"' applied to the <directories> tag containing the target file path in the Wazuh configuration.

task_completion.description: Apply the new FIM configuration by restarting the Wazuh manager.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal command restarting the Wazuh manager, such as '/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-control restart' or restarting the wazuh-wazuh.manager-1 docker container.

task_completion.description: Modify the content of the target file to trigger a FIM alert.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a terminal command modifying the content of '/var/ossec/etc/critical_app.conf', such as using 'echo' to append text or editing it with a text editor.

task_completion.description: Verify that the generated FIM alert includes the exact content difference.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a log output (e.g., tailing /var/ossec/logs/alerts/alerts.json or alerts.log) or the Wazuh dashboard showing a syscheck alert for the target file that explicitly includes the 'syscheck.diff' field or shows the exact content changes.

integrity.description: Verify that the configuration changes were made to the actual ossec.conf file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the configuration edits are shown inside a genuine text editor interface (nano, vim) or via valid shell commands, rather than just being typed out as a standalone text block.

integrity.description: Verify that the target file was actually modified in the system.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the file modification was executed via a valid terminal command (e.g., echo, sed, or a text editor) inside the container, rather than just simulated or stated.

integrity.description: Verify that the FIM alert showing the diff comes from an actual Wazuh log or dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the alert output is displayed by reading an actual log file (like alerts.json) using commands like cat, tail, or grep, or viewed in the Wazuh UI, rather than a manually crafted JSON object printed to the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target container: wazuh-wazuh.manager-1. Target file to monitor: /var/ossec/etc/critical_app.conf. Required FIM attributes in ossec.conf: report_changes="yes" and realtime="yes". The VLM must verify that the final alert contains the exact content difference, typically represented by the 'syscheck.diff' field in the alert JSON.
